items.title: OKC Thunder trade wing Lu Dort to Atlanta in three-team deal
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder are trading Lu Dort to the Atlanta Hawks in a three-team trade, ESPN's Shams Charania reported on Sunday, July 19.OKC gets back three second-round draft picks from the Hawks in exchange for Dort; Atlanta is also sending guard Ryan Nembard to the Dallas Mavericks for former No. 1 overall pick Zaccharie Risacher.With this trade, the Thunder have now acquired seven second-round picks this offseason after previously trading Aaron Wiggins to the Hawks and Isaiah Joe to the Detroit Pistons for two second-round picks apiece. Dort had a $17.7 million team option and rather than declining it to save cap space and let him walk for nothing, general manager Sam Presti decided to pick it up and get some value for the first-team All-Defensive wing.Now, Dort is in a position to get the long-term contract he wouldn't have been able to in OKC and will reunite with Wiggins in…

items.title: Thunder reportedly trading Lu Dort to Atlanta in three-team deal with Dallas
items.summary: Some trades are a win-win for the key teams involved, and that is the case with Oklahoma City and Atlanta.The Thunder have agreed to trade All-Defensive wing Lu Dort to the Atlanta Hawks, Oklahoma City gets three second-round picks, and, as part of the deal, Atlanta will send Zaccharie Risacher to Dallas while Ryan Nembhard heads to Atlanta, a trade broken by Shams Charania of ESPN. One of the three second-round picks is Chicago's in 2027 (which Dallas controlled), while the second-round picks going from Atlanta to OKC (2031 and 2032) are reportedly top 55 protected.For Oklahoma City, this was all about saving money and getting below the second apron of the luxury tax. With the max contract extensions for Chet Holmgren and Jalen Williams kicking in next season, plus the team having reached a new deal with Isaiah Hartenstein, the Thunder have been looking to shed salary all summer…

items.title: Thunder trade Lu Dort to Hawks; former No. 1 pick Zaccharie Risacher heads to Mavericks in 3-team deal
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder are trading forward Lu Dort to the Atlanta Hawks, per ESPN's Shams Charania. It is a three-team deal that will see former No. 1 pick Zaccharie Risacher head to the Dallas Mavericks and three second-round picks going back to Oklahoma City. Here are the complete trade terms:Thunder receive:3 second-round picksMavericks receive:F Zaccharie RisacherHawks receive:F Lu DortG Ryan Nembhard Dort has been in the league since 2019. The 27-year-old had spent his entire career with the Thunder, winning the 2025 NBA Finals. Dort is known for his defense, and he was named to the NBA's All-Defensive First Team in 2025. The forward played in 69 games last season and averaged 8.3 points, 3.6 rebounds and 1.2 assists in 26.8 minutes per game. Risacher was the first overall pick in the 2024 NBA Draft by Atlanta. The forward played internationally leading up to the draft.…
